new_posts.title: GitHub Enterprise Server 3.21 is now generally available
new_posts.summary: GitHub Enterprise Server (GHES) 3.21 enhances deployment efficiency, monitoring capabilities, code security, and policy management. Here are a few highlights in the 3.21 release: Organization custo

new_posts.title: Bot-created pull requests can run workflows if approved
new_posts.summary: Pull requests created by the github-actions[bot] are now able to run your CI/CD workflows with user approval. Requiring approval is a security measure to ensure generated code does not automaticall

new_posts.title: AI usage report updates
new_posts.summary: Your AI usage reports now reflect GitHub AI Credits usage in the standard report fields. To monitor AI credit usage going forward, use quantity for AI credit quantity and gross_amount…

new_posts.title: Copilot CLI: Configure everything from one place with /settings
new_posts.summary: GitHub Copilot CLI now has a unified, schema-driven home for configuration. The new /settings slash command combines the scattered commands like /theme, /streamer-mode, and /experimental with optio

new_posts.title: New runner images in public preview
new_posts.summary: Two new GitHub-hosted runner images for GitHub Actions are now available in public preview for all users, giving you early access to test your workflows on the latest platforms before…

new_posts.title: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview
new_posts.summary: GitHub Agentic Workflows is now in public preview. With agentic workflows, you can automate reasoning-based tasks like issue triage, CI failure analysis, and documentation updates by leveraging cod

new_posts.title: Agentic workflows no longer need a personal access token
new_posts.summary: You can now use GitHub Agentic Workflows with GitHub Actions’s built-in GITHUB_TOKEN. This means that you no longer need to create and store a personal access token (PAT), eliminating theR

new_posts.title: Manage sub-issues, types, and dependencies from GitHub CLI
new_posts.summary: GitHub CLI now exposes issue types, parent and sub-issue relationships, and issue dependencies directly from the terminal. This means you can structure and track work without dropping into the brow

new_posts.title: Copilot Chat now sees your agent sessions
new_posts.summary: We’ve improved the handoff experience between Copilot Chat and Copilot cloud agent on the web. We’ve also enabled new functionality which allows you to search and query past agent sessi

new_posts.title: Give GitHub Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers
new_posts.summary:
Install and configure LSP servers for GitHub Copilot CLI, replacing brute-force grep/decompile with real code intelligence.

The post

new_posts.title: From one-off prompts to workflows: How to use custom agents in GitHub Copilot CLI
new_posts.summary:
Custom agents let GitHub Copilot CLI understand your stack and team workflows, turning one-off terminal prompts into repeatable, reviewable processes.

The post
